_meta.description: OntoMarket precision/recall eval set — 5 preset questions with expected entities and hop counts.
_meta.scoring: Graph entity recall = fraction of must_include_entities found in any result column. Vector entity recall = fraction found across top-5 snippet texts. Vector chain recall = 1 if any single snippet contains ALL must_include_entities, else 0 (multi-hop chains never collapse into one sentence).

questions.question: Which companies face supply chain exposure given an executive moved from a company to its competitor after a major restructuring event?
questions.hop_chain: Event → AFFECTED_BY → Company → COMPETES_WITH → Company ← EXECUTIVE_OF ← Person → EXECUTIVE_OF (current) and Company ← SUPPLIES_TO ← Supplier

must_include_rationale.MSFT: affected company — has EVT-MSFT-2023-openai, EVT-MSFT-2023-activision, EVT-INTC-2024-foundry-msft
must_include_rationale.AAPL: competitor — Amar Subramanya moved MSFT→AAPL (start 2025-12-01, end null); MSFT-AAPL COMPETES_WITH exists

questions.vector_limitation: No single snippet states: 'MSFT was affected by an event, Amar Subramanya moved to its competitor AAPL, and NVDA/INTC supply to MSFT'. The 4-hop chain cannot be expressed in one sentence.

questions.question: An executive moved from a company to a competitor — has that competitor had a notable event since the move?
questions.hop_chain: Person → EXECUTIVE_OF (former, end_date not null) → Company → COMPETES_WITH → Company ← AFFECTED_BY ← Event (after move date)

must_include_rationale.SNOW: current company — Sridhar Ramaswamy moved from GOOGL to SNOW (start 2024-02-28, end null)
must_include_rationale.GOOGL: former company — Sridhar was SVP at GOOGL (end 2018-01-01); GOOGL-? or MSFT-GOOGL competes
must_include_rationale.EVT-SNOW-2024-ceo: event at SNOW (CEO transition) which occurs after the exec move date

questions.vector_limitation: Vector cannot chain: find who left GOOGL, confirm they joined SNOW, then find events at SNOW that post-date the move. Three separate lookups that require date-ordered traversal.

questions.question: If a company faces a regulatory action or lawsuit, which of its suppliers face downstream exposure?
questions.hop_chain: Company → AFFECTED_BY → Event (regulatory_filing or lawsuit) and Company ← SUPPLIES_TO ← Supplier

must_include_rationale.NVDA: NVDA AFFECTED_BY EVT-NVDA-2023-export-controls (regulatory_filing); TSM supplies to NVDA
must_include_rationale.GOOGL: GOOGL AFFECTED_BY EVT-GOOGL-2024-antitrust (lawsuit); AVGO supplies to GOOGL
must_include_rationale.INTC: INTC AFFECTED_BY EVT-INTC-2024-restructure (regulatory_filing); who supplies INTC?

questions.vector_limitation: Vector retrieves 'NVDA faces export controls' and 'TSM makes NVDA chips' as separate snippets but cannot join them to assert supply exposure.

questions.question: What notable events have Intel's direct competitors had recently?

must_include_rationale.AMD: INTC COMPETES_WITH AMD; AMD has EVT-AMD-2023-mi300x
must_include_rationale.NVDA: INTC COMPETES_WITH NVDA; NVDA has EVT-NVDA-2023-export-controls

questions.vector_limitation: Vector can find 'AMD launched MI300X' and 'NVDA faces export controls' independently, but cannot guarantee completeness — it won't know which companies are INTC's graph-defined competitors unless it happens to retrieve those snippets in top-5.

questions.question: Which companies supply AI chips and infrastructure to the major cloud providers?

must_include_rationale.NVDA: supplies H100 GPUs to MSFT, AMZN, GOOGL, META, ORCL
must_include_rationale.MSFT: customer — receives from NVDA and INTC
must_include_rationale.GOOGL: customer — receives from NVDA and AVGO
must_include_rationale.TSM: upstream fab — supplies to NVDA, AMD, AAPL, AVGO
must_include_rationale.AVGO: supplies custom ASICs to GOOGL and META

questions.vector_limitation: Vector can retrieve individual supply relationships but will miss some and cannot guarantee a complete map — it has no concept of 'all' suppliers.
